content.rendered:
1967: The Castiles play a show at Freehold Regional High School.

1971: The Bruce Springsteen Band spends Thanksgiving Eve in residence at the Student Prince in Asbury Park.

1974: Bruce takes the bus from Asbury Park down to Philadelphia to spend a couple of days with local DJ Ed Sciaky, starting with a visit to a local studio the next morning to meet David Bowie.

1975: Bruce makes a return visit to the Hammersmith Odeon in London a week after his debut performance. Bruce tour premieres “Wear My Ring Around Your Neck” for the occasion, and he closes the show with the last “Little Queenie” for almost a quarter-century. This show is available as part of Bruce’s official archive series.

1980: Night Two at the Capital Center in Largo on the River Tour is filmed (not by Springsteen’s crew, but by sneaky employees at the venue).

1992: Bruce spends another day in Florida, travelling from Orlando to Miami to perform a show that evening at the Miami Arena.

1996: Bruce kicks off Year Two(!) of the Ghost of Tom Joad Tour with a trio of shows at the Paramount Theater in Asbury Park. Each show is special–a homecoming of sorts, with guest stars and tour premieres galore. On this, Night One, Bruce is joined at various times throughout the night by Patti, Soozie, and Danny. Bruce tour premieres “Independence Day,” “Wild Billy’s Circus Story,” “When You’re Alone,” “4th of July, Asbury Park (Sandy),” and (remarkably) a wild, acoustic, “Rosalita (Come Out Tonight).” Kicking off with three tracks from Greetings and wrapping up with a pair from Wild & Innocent (before the customary “The Promised Land” finale), this certainly doesn’t feel like the Ghost of Tom Joad Tour. This show is available as part of Bruce’s official archive series.

2002: Bruce and the E Street Band wrap up a week-long tour of Florida with a show at the Ice Palace in Tampa, where Bruce closes the show with a tour premiere of “Twist and Shout.”

2017: Springsteen on Broadway continues its theatrical run at the Walter Kerr Theater in New York City.

excerpt.rendered: This date in Springsteen history gave us some gems: check out the unreleased 1980 concert video from Largo, listen to highlights from Bruce’s trio of Asbury Park concerts in 1996, and more.

jetpack-related-posts.title: November 26
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: On this date: Bruce makes a guest appearance with Patti Smith, debuts "Hearts of Stone," and records a Christmas greeting for Ed Sciaky. Plus: a very crowded "solo" stage in Asbury Park and much more.

jetpack-related-posts.title: September 17
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: On this date: Born to Run hits #1, Bruce debuts his duet with Sting on "Every Breath You Take," and a tantalizing audio glimpse of a still-unreleased, never-performed original song.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Kingdom of Days: December 3
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: Today in Springsteen history: Bruce releases two new videos, a star-studded holiday show in Asbury Park features the return of some notable set list prodigals, and an epic 20-minute "Kitty's Back." More highlights inside.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Kingdom of Days: December 19
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: A holiday SNL appearance, Bruce's first-ever Canadian show, and two raucous holiday shows lead off today's highlights.

jetpack-related-posts.title: October 1
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: The Vote for Change Tour kicks off, The Rising Tour winds down, and Bruce kicks off a mini-tour of Italy with the Sessions Band. Plus: Bruce serenades Sting on his 60th birthday, and I meet Bruce in Seattle!

jetpack-related-posts.title: November 13
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: On this date: The debut of "The Hitter," a rare 1971 soundboard performance, and more. Plus: "Good evening, Ohio!"
